meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't deploy apps (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't deploy apps. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template to Solve Deployment
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to launch your first professional-grade student app today.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template solves deployment bottlenecks by abstracting infrastructure complexity into a standardized, production-ready scaffold. By pairing this with Claude Code, you automate the environment configuration and CI/CD pipelines, allowing you to bypass manual server management and ship your student projects directly to live production environments seamlessly.
content.intro:
You’ve spent days grinding through the logic, perfecting your React components, and feeling the rush of a working local build. Then, you try to deploy. Suddenly, you're buried in a graveyard of environment variables, build errors, and cryptic CLI logs that make you question if your code actually works. The gap between a local prototype and a live URL is where most student dreams go to die.

But it doesn't have to be this way. The Antigravity project template acts as your architectural blueprint, enforcing best practices from line one. When combined with the raw power of Claude Code, you aren't just writing code—you're orchestrating a fully automated deployment pipeline. It’s time to stop debugging server configurations and start shipping products that actually exist on the internet.

steps.title: Initialize with Antigravity
steps.description: Start by cloning the boilerplate to ensure your project structure is optimized for modern cloud providers, eliminating configuration drift.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity template for a Full-Stack TypeScript application with automatic Vercel deployment hooks.

steps.title: Automate Environment Secrets
steps.description: Never hardcode keys again. Use Claude Code to map your local .env variables to the cloud provider's secure secret store.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my local .env file and generate the necessary commands to securely inject these into the production environment via the CLI.

steps.title: Configure CI/CD Pipelines
steps.description: Ensure your code passes tests before it goes live. This step automates the integration of GitHub Actions within your template.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow inside the .github/workflows directory that runs linting and build checks on every push.

steps.title: Pre-flight Production Check
steps.description: Run a final audit to ensure your build artifacts are optimized and no sensitive data is exposed in the public directory.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current project for production-readiness, specifically checking for exposed secrets and bundle size optimization.

steps.title: Final Deployment Sync
steps.description: Push your code to the remote repository and trigger the first automated deployment to your live URL.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Commit all changes with a professional message and push to the main branch to trigger the production deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'feat: initial production deploy' && git push origin main

pro_tips: Always use the --dry-run flag in your deployment scripts to catch infrastructure errors before they hit your cloud provider.
Structure your project components by feature, not by type, to make Claude Code's context-window processing significantly more accurate.
Implement a 'pre-build' script that strips out console.log statements to keep your production logs clean and professional.
Use distinct environment prefixes (e.g., DEV_, PROD_) even within your local files to prevent accidental overwrites during deployment.
Leverage the Antigravity 'health-check' endpoint to automatically ping your site every 5 minutes, preventing cold-start latency.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, iterate by adding a real-time database like Supabase and monitoring user traffic with PostHog to gather actual product data.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to move from 'student coder' to 'shipped engineer.' Don't let your code sit idle on a local machine. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
